channel.title: Nas on Pi Stack
channel.description: Recent content in Nas on Pi Stack

item.title: MiniDLNA vs Gerbera vs Rygel: Best Self-Hosted DLNA/UPnP Media Servers 2026
item.description: DLNA (Digital Living Network Alliance) and UPnP (Universal Plug and Play) protocols let you stream media from a central server to any compatible device on your network — smart TVs, game consoles, media players, and mobile apps. Instead of relying on cloud services, a self-hosted DLNA server gives you full control over your media library with zero subscription fees.

item.title: OpenMediaVault vs TrueNAS SCALE vs Rockstor: Best Self-Hosted NAS Solutions 2026
item.description: A Network Attached Storage (NAS) device is the backbone of any self-hosted home lab or small business infrastructure. Whether you are centralizing file backups, running media servers, or managing shared project files, choosing the right NAS operating system determines how easy it is to manage your storage long-term.
